description: This is a conceptual grid diagram depicting the spatial distribution of scatterer types within a 10x10 grid of cells. The background cells display a colour gradient ranging from deep blue (representing lower signal intensity) to lighter blue, then yellow, and a central red cell (representing peak signal intensity). This gradient likely illustrates the intensity or value of a Persistent Scatterer (PS) signal or an averaged Distributed Scatterer (DS) signal. Eight orange triangular markers are superimposed on the grid. These markers represent individual Distributed Scatterer (DS) points. Four orange triangles are located in the yellow cells immediately adjacent to the central red cell (top, bottom, left, right). The other four orange triangles are situated in deep blue cells further from the centre: one top-centre, one bottom-centre, one middle-left, and one middle-right. The image caption mentions "PS (blue) and 3 families of DSs (orange, green, and grey)", but only orange DS points are visually represented on the PS-like background signal gradient.
